observations: Lines 3-11: opening threat scan lands in Sylvanas's clipped tactical cadence; the short commands and environmental hooks match the chapter's opening-gambit spec.
Lines 27-29: the house read tipped into one fragment too many; I merged the conclusion so the paragraph still snaps without turning telegraphic.
Lines 45-49: the lane inventory now resolves in one cleaner sentence before Cyndia re-enters, which keeps the catalog from flattening into uniform short beats.
Lines 105-109: the churned-ground read had a dense run of micro-sentences; I combined two beats and one inference to restore a needed longer pulse.
Lines 311-317: the north-road spoor now includes one orienting sentence before Jaina's confirmation, preserving pressure while reducing fragment stacking.

fixes.rule: sentenceRhythmStandards.default — fragment stack exceeded the chapter's baseline cadence
fixes.before: Dust settling on the sill. Not much. Enough. Interrupted. Not cleared.
fixes.after: Dust settling on the sill. Not much. Enough to tell her the meal had been broken off, not cleared away.

fixes.rule: sentenceRhythmStandards.action/default — terrain read needed one longer orienting sentence
fixes.before: Dirt broken to clods. Old mud kicked up under new. Drag marks. Heel gouges.
fixes.after: Dirt broken to clods, old mud kicked up under new. Drag marks and heel gouges.

fixes.rule: sentenceRhythmStandards.introspection — inference chain was too chopped to carry causality cleanly
fixes.before: Not a clean column of tracks. Too much overlap. Too much churn. But the story held. A great deal of movement away from the village. Not carts. Not panicked flight. Feet that dragged, crossed, wandered, then found one pull and followed it.
fixes.after: No clean column of tracks. Too much overlap, too much churn. But the story held. A great deal of movement away from the village: feet dragging, crossing, wandering, then finding one pull and following it.

fixes.rule: sentenceRhythmStandards.default — repeated three-word fragments needed consolidation
fixes.before: Bare feet. Boot heels. Dragging hems.
fixes.after: Bare feet, boot heels, dragging hems.

observations: Lines 15-19: the magical detail stays field-plain — dim crystal, banked blue, no lab-report diction.
Lines 75-93: Jaina's granary explanation uses ordinary causality words ('bell,' 'wound,' 'grain,' 'stain') instead of technocratic jargon.
Lines 123-133: her answer about the churn stays in usable soldier-facing language — 'what they disturbed' rather than abstract arcane terminology.
Line 317: I removed the residual negation scaffold from Jaina's road read so the line lands as plain witness language, not rhetorical setup.
Lines 349-353: the warning note is diegetic dispatch prose, so its clipped field shorthand reads as character function rather than narration contamination.

fixes.rule: vocabularyStandards.baseline / anti-slop negation addiction — land on the concrete noun instead of the scaffold
fixes.before: "Most of it runs this way," the mage said. "Not carts. Bodies. Many together. They kept to the road once they found it."
fixes.after: "Most of it runs this way," the mage said. "Bodies. Many together. They kept to the road once they found it."

observations: Line 15: 'like banked fire' is a single clean image tied to contained danger; it does useful work and does not stack.
Lines 89-90: the bell-and-wound comparison clarifies signal versus source in one stroke and stays inside Jaina's explanatory mode.
Line 171: 'blue enough to sour the dark' gives contamination texture without blooming into decorative spectacle.
Line 313: 'looked used up' is lean and serviceable, closer to field judgment than ornamental lyricism.
Lines 497-509: the close keeps imagery sparse; no epic abstractions, no mixed domains, no back-to-back simile pileup.
Lines 3-23: opening paragraphs mostly stay within the 1-4 sentence target, with one-line beats used for dread and command turns.
Lines 27-29: the interior-house conclusion had one extra isolated fragment; compressing it restored a cleaner paragraph finish.
Line 45: I collapsed the paired absence fragments into one sentence so the paragraph ends with sharper pressure instead of two matching taps.
Lines 239-291: Mira's questioning sequence breaks every 1-2 sentences, which suits interrogation pacing and keeps the child-response beats clear.
Lines 343-353: the proof threshold moves straight into the written warning, so scene transition happens by consequence rather than reset.

fixes.rule: paragraphLengthGuidelines.sceneBalance — end-of-paragraph fragment pairing risked monotony
fixes.before: No blood worth naming. No bodies.
fixes.after: No blood worth naming and no bodies.

observations: Overall ratio measured 31.7:68.3 dialogue-to-narration, which fits a dread-heavy investigation chapter with interrogation pockets.
Lines 165-231: the cellar rescue swings dialogue-heavy at the right moment, then drops back into concrete reaction beats.
Said/asked-style tags dominate the chapter; stronger tags ('murmured,' 'whispered,' 'snorted') stay sparse and situational.
Lines 185-209: Neral's guidance uses dialogue plus action placement after the line, aligning care, status, and readability cleanly.
Lines 407-477: the Paret exchange stays readable with short answer blocks, minimal tags, and no creative-tag overreach.
Lines 3-11: Sylvanas notices ground, silence, movement, and threat geometry first; the scene opens exactly through her filter.
Across the chapter, Jaina remains 'the mage' in Sylvanas-facing narration, preserving the chapter-3 relationship phase rule.
Lines 105-111: Sylvanas processes spoor as threat assessment to conclusion, not as abstract meditation.
Lines 239-295: Mira's testimony is handled as utility, proof, and consequence; emotion stays present through bodily detail rather than soft-focus pity.
Lines 499-513: Sylvanas's final acknowledgement of Jaina registers as operational recalculation, not intimacy or overt admiration.
Adverbs per page measured 0.7, well below the hard cap of 4.
Passive-voice heuristic measured 0.7%, well below the 4% ceiling; the few candidates are not rhythm-breaking.
Showing dominates: empty houses, churned earth, Mira's body language, and the road spoor all carry information without explanatory gloss.
Filter-word and academic-register sweeps came back clean; no 'noticed/felt/saw' clutter and no banned systems-talk survived.
Simile load stays controlled: one clear 'like' simile of note, no 'as if' constructions, no comparison fog.
Exposition integration stays organic at lines 75-133 and 245-291; world facts arrive through inspection, translation, and questioning under pressure.
Power dynamics track cleanly: Sylvanas's clipped imperatives dominate lines 17-21, 97-103, and 385-397, while Jaina's longer replies mark explanatory resistance.
The scene-shape midpoint turn lands when Mira emerges at lines 181-211, shifting the governing question from absence to confirmation.
Speculative integration is highly domesticated: magic reads as residue, strain, contamination, and proof threshold rather than spectacle.
Humor deployment is compliant: Neral, Jaina, and Paret each get one dry pressure-valve line, but the horror beats themselves stay straight.

fingerprintChecks.vocabularyLevel: pass — lines 3-11, 45-49, and 349-355 use lean martial diction, rank-aware commands, and concrete threat language with no soft or modern contamination.
fingerprintChecks.sentenceRhythm: pass — short declaratives still dominate, but lines 27, 105, 109, and 313 were smoothed so the clipped cadence stops short of monotone telegraphese.
fingerprintChecks.metaphorDensity: pass — imagery remains sparse and martial-horror adjacent; the chapter never drifts into ornamental lyricism.
fingerprintChecks.emotionalRegister: pass — emotion appears as irritation, proof-threshold pressure, and operational urgency rather than named feeling.
fingerprintChecks.thoughtPatterns: pass — the chapter repeatedly moves from read to judgment to decision: house scan, granary, churn, survivor testimony, road spoor, warning dispatch.
fingerprintChecks.sensorySignature: pass — silence, footing, movement, sightlines, ground-read, and exposure stay foregrounded throughout.

ruleViolations: Fixed lines 27 and 105-109 where fragment density briefly over-cranked Sylvanas's natural clipped cadence.
No intimacy drift detected: Jaina never becomes 'Jaina' in Sylvanas's interior narration in this chapter.
